title: System tests: big glop o' cleanup
body:
One of the system tests was creating a volume and not cleaning up
after itself. Fix that: do cleanup in the test itself. And, add
a 'volume rm -af' to global teardown() to leave things clean for
the next tests.

While testing on a VM without `/usr/bin/podman`, found a bunch
of cases where system tests were invoking `podman` or `/usr/bin/podman`;
replaced those with `run_podman` or `$PODMAN` as appropriate.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Update: OOPS! Two system tests were incorrectly running `podman` instead of `run_podman`. Fixed and re-pushed.
UGH! One more: a `run <space> podman` that should've been `run_podman`. Fixed and re-pushed again.
Should we rm podman from $PATH in the system tests CI task to make sure they are using only the correct version?
That's a really good idea. I think `/usr/bin/podman` is used in various parts of CI, for tasks other than tests, but I'm going to try it anyway and see what (if anything) breaks. Thanks,
Submitted #16309. Fingers crossed. Thanks @Luap99 for the suggestion.
